enonce.texte: Read each sentence carefully. Choose the correct word or expression related to community development to fill in the blank.

questions.texte: The local population decided to build a new well. This is an example of a(n) ________ project.

sousQuestions.texte: A. individual profit B. community development C. business investment D. political campaign

questions.texte: After the storm, the villagers organized a ________ to clean the roads and remove fallen trees.

sousQuestions.texte: A. fundraising B. clean-up C. awareness campaign D. meeting

questions.texte: To build a new school, the parents' association started a ________ to collect money.

sousQuestions.texte: A. construction B. clean-up C. fundraising campaign D. protest

questions.texte: The NGO organized an ________ to teach people about the dangers of malaria.

sousQuestions.texte: A. election B. awareness campaign C. infrastructure project D. business fair

questions.texte: Building roads, bridges, and hospitals is part of improving a community's ________.

sousQuestions.texte: A. entertainment B. infrastructure C. administration D. agriculture

enonce.texte: Read the case study about a Cameroonian village and answer the questions that follow. Use Bloom's taxonomy verbs to demonstrate your understanding.

questions.texte:
Case Study: The village of Nkolbisson has a serious problem. The only river that provides water is polluted because people wash clothes and throw garbage there. Many children get sick from drinking this water. The chief wants to solve this problem.

IDENTIFY the main problem in Nkolbisson.
questions.reponse: The main problem is water pollution from the river, causing sickness in children.

questions.texte: LIST three possible community development actions that could solve this water problem.
questions.reponse: 1. Organize a clean-up campaign to remove garbage from the river. 2. Launch an awareness campaign to teach people not to wash clothes in the river. 3. Fundraise to build a well or install a water purification system.

questions.texte: EXPLAIN why an awareness campaign alone might not be enough to solve the problem.
questions.reponse: An awareness campaign educates people, but it does not provide an alternative. If people have no other place to wash clothes or get water, they might continue polluting the river. Therefore, infrastructure (like a public tap or washing area) is also necessary.

questions.texte: DESIGN a small community project for Nkolbisson. Your answer must include: (a) the name of the project, (b) three specific activities, and (c) the expected result.
questions.reponse:
(a) 'Clean Water for Nkolbisson' project.
(b) Activities: 1. Organize a weekly clean-up of the river. 2. Build a protected well with a hand pump. 3. Train two villagers as water monitors.
(c) Expected result: Reduced cases of waterborne diseases and access to safe drinking water for all families.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences using the correct expression of purpose: in order to, so as to, in order not to, so as not to.

questions.texte: The villagers formed a committee ________ coordinate the clean-up campaign.
They collected money ________ build a new community hall.
The chief spoke loudly ________ be heard by everyone at the meeting.
They avoided using plastic bags ________ pollute the environment further.
The NGO provided mosquito nets ________ prevent malaria.

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue and answer the questions that follow.

Chief Martin: 'Our school has no benches. The children sit on the floor.'
Mrs. Atangana: 'We must do something. Let's organize a fundraising event.'
Chief Martin: 'Good idea. We can also ask the local sawmill for wood.'
Mr. Njoya: 'I am a carpenter. I can build the benches for free.'
Mrs. Atangana: 'Excellent! Let's create a development committee.'

questions.texte: IDENTIFY the community problem mentioned in the dialogue.
questions.reponse: The school has no benches; children sit on the floor.

questions.texte: LIST three resources or people mentioned that can help solve this problem.
questions.reponse: 1. Chief Martin (leader/initiator), 2. Mrs. Atangana (organizer), 3. Mr. Njoya (carpenter/skill provider), 4. Local sawmill (wood/material).

questions.texte: ANALYZE why creating a 'development committee' is important for this project.
questions.reponse: A development committee organizes tasks, manages funds, assigns responsibilities, and ensures transparency. Without a committee, efforts might be uncoordinated, money could be mismanaged, and the project might fail. It also allows for shared decision-making.

questions.texte: JUDGE the importance of Mr. Njoya's contribution. What would happen if he were not involved?
questions.reponse: Mr. Njoya's contribution is very important because he offers free skilled labor. If he were not involved, the community would have to pay a carpenter, which would increase the cost. They might not be able to afford the benches, and the project would be delayed or cancelled.
